content.text:
Apply golden section search to find the minimum of $f(x) = x^2 - 4x + 5$ 
on $[0, 5]$. Perform 4 iterations and compare with the exact minimum.

Find the minimum of $f(x) = e^x - 4x$ on $[1, 2]$ using golden section 
search. How many iterations are needed for accuracy $\varepsilon = 0.01$?

Verify that $\rho = (3 - \sqrt{5})/2$ satisfies the self-similarity 
equation $\rho = (1 - \rho)^2$ derived in the golden section analysis.

Compare the convergence rates of golden section search and bisection 
for root-finding. Which requires more iterations for the same accuracy?
